principles: Source adapters run before C2 and return normalized seed rows only.
C2 remains the official website scraper and evidence layer.
No-website leads stay in seeded_research_needed or research_needed lanes.
Promotion toward pulled requires durable match evidence: website, Google Place ID, phone, address, official license ID, or official entity ID.
Public social/context links may improve scoring and routing but must not become unsupported personalization.

recommended_first_adapters.reason: Current dealership website-first discovery has the weakest verified-email yield; OEM locators, group pages, platform footprints, and license rosters create better rooftop seeds before C2.
Places-style and reservation/delivery profiles create broad, high-volume restaurant seeds with POS, ordering, reservation, catering, social, and multi-location clues.
License and network directories give higher-confidence independent pharmacy seeds, active-status checks, and owner/pharmacist evidence before website scrape.

ranked_sources.why: Official brand locators are high-trust rooftop discovery with brand, address, phone, website, and sometimes service links.
Group pages reveal ownership structure, multi-rooftop operators, executive/contact pages, and decision-maker signals.
Platform footprints often expose website stack, service/scheduler/payment flows, and consistent rooftop metadata.
Aggregator listings are good coverage backstops with phone, address, brand, inventory, and profile URLs.
License lists validate active status and legal entity/rooftop existence, especially for no-website seeds.
Associations can surface owners, executives, and regional operators when official websites are thin.
Broadest coverage for restaurant discovery with phone, address, website, ratings, reviews, and Place ID dedupe.
Reservation profiles signal higher operational complexity and guest-flow workflows.
Ordering/delivery profiles reveal online ordering, delivery, POS/payment, and guest-data fragmentation clues.
Associations can surface owner/operator names, member status, and regional independent operators.
Local official directories add coverage for independents and no-website operators.
Aggregator/editorial lists are useful for local expansion but lower provenance than official or profile sources.
Official license status and license IDs give the strongest seed provenance and prevent closed/expired pharmacies from entering active outreach.
Independent pharmacy membership context improves owner/operator and pharmacy-type confidence.
Network directories reveal independent/community pharmacy operators and often include profile pages.
CPESN participation can signal service-oriented pharmacy workflows and operational maturity.
Association directories can expose owner/pharmacist evidence and active local operators.
Local directories are fallback coverage only; they need stronger matching before C2 promotion.

confidence_rules.high: Website or Google Place ID plus phone or address; suitable as a C2 candidate after resolver review.
confidence_rules.medium: Official license/entity ID plus phone or address, or phone plus address; keep as seed until website or profile match is resolved.
confidence_rules.low: Only one durable match field; keep in research_needed.
confidence_rules.research_needed: No durable match fields; preserve as a seed but do not promote.

dedupe_keys: website domain after resolver/C2 confirms it

expected_impact.email_supply: Higher top-of-funnel seed volume because Outreach stops waiting for business websites to be the discovery source.
expected_impact.decision_maker_confidence: Improves where group pages, association profiles, license rosters, and staff/contact pages expose owner, operator, pharmacist, GM, or executive signals.
expected_impact.enrichment_quality: Raises verified conversion by giving C2 better website/profile targets and by preserving no-website leads for resolver work instead of discarding them.
